PROGRAM SCOPE
With most of the electronics and communication systems being driven by software today, there is an imminent need for the Electronics & Communication Engineer to be well versed with software too.
The Program of Computer & Communication Engineering at Sri Eshwar offers the students with an excellent combination of electronics & communication engineering and software.
This dual advantage expands the scope of career progression for the students in electronics, communication and IT companies.
LEARNING SCOPE
CCE is a dual advantage program where the students, acquire software skills on par with CSE students along with ECE skills.
The curriculum of CCE imparts the strong fundamental knowledge on electronic courses including Digital systems, Communication Technologies, Embedded Systems, and IoT, and on computer related courses like C, Data Structures, Python & Java programming, Data base management systems, Object Oriented Programming, Networking & Cybersecurity, Web & Mobile application Development.
CAREER PROGRESSION
The students pursuing Computer and Communication Engineering (CCE) will have the dual opportunity of getting placed in core communication and electronics companies or in leading IT companies. There is a great demand for fresh engineers with both software and communication engineering knowledge.
VISION
To emerge as a pioneering department, nurturing students into futuristic and globally competent Computer and Communication Engineering professionals.
MISSION
To offer high-quality engineering education emphasizing practical outcomes.
To foster innovative research and project development among students and instil a sense of social responsibility.
To equip students with industry-relevant skills and robust leadership qualities through pedagogical methodologies and industry engagement.
To inculcate a culture of socially relevant values and nurture moral, ethical, and interpersonal skills among students.
PROGRAM DETAILS
Program Educational Objectives
PEO1:
Acquire core competencies in computational technologies and communication engineering with the ability to utilise the latest software tools to effectively address real time challenges.
PEO2:
Active involvement in research, innovation, or entrepreneurial ventures, resulting in significant societal contributions.
PEO3:
Demonstrate industry-relevant skills, effective leadership qualities, and strong ethical values to exhibit professionalism and foster collaborative work experience.
Program Outcomes
PO1:
Engineering Knowledge:
Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization to develop the solution of complex engineering problems.
PO2:
Problem Analysis:
Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development.
PO3:
Design/Development of Solutions:
Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society, and environment as required.
PO4:
Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems:
Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions.
PO5:
Engineering Tool Usage:
Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems.
PO6:
The Engineer and The World:
Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture, and environment.
PO7:
Ethics:
Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws.
PO8:
Individual and Collaborative Teamwork:
Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams.
PO9:
Communication:
Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences.
PO10:
Project Management and Finance:
Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.
PO11:
Life-Long Learning:
Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change.
Program Specific Outcomes
PSO1:
Apply the concepts of computer and communication systems to develop software for different applications.
PSO2:
Acquire skills and knowledge to solve real time challenges in communication networks.

This greatly helps us bring in the industry-perspective into the courses being taught and a hands-on learning for the students

Dr.R.Deepa
B.E, M.E, Ph.D.
D.O.J : 09.05.2025
Areas of Specialization
Wireless communication
Expert in Signal Processing | Researcher in Advanced Communication Systems | Academic Leader | Mentor
With over 20 years of experience in academia and research, I specialize in high-performance, low-complexity signal processing algorithms, software-defined radio, and image transmission. My work bridges innovation and education—spanning doctoral mentorship, curriculum design, and extensive publications in reputed journals. I’m passionate about fostering research-driven learning, driving interdisciplinary collaboration, and contributing as an editor, peer reviewer, and subject matter expert across global academic platforms.
Dr.C.Ganesh
M.E, (Ph.D)
D.O.J : 24.02.2021
Areas of Specialization
Natural Language Processing, AI and Machine Learning
Dr.C.Ganesh has a strong academic and professional background in engineering and research. With a B.E. in Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering in 2008, an M.E. in Applied Electronics in the year of 2013, and a Ph.D. in Information and Communication in the year of 2022, Dr. C Ganesh has built a solid foundation in the communication field. Dr C. Ganesh started his career as a software engineer at Hexaware Technologies, Chennai, from 2011 to 2013. He has contributed to research with 5 SCI publications, 15 Scopus-indexed conference papers, and 3 patents. He is currently guiding 10 undergraduate batches and 2 postgraduate students and he has also earned 200 Scopus citations and obtained certifications from NPTEL, E-Box, and Udemy. As a member of ISTE, IETE, and IAENG, he actively participates in professional activities and has submitted various research proposals to ICMR, MNRE, SERB, and AU.
Dr.S.Dhamodharan
M.E, (Ph.D)
D.O.J : 22.07.2022
Areas of Specialization
Wireless Communication
Dr. S. Dhamodharan holds a distinguished academic background, with a BE in ECE (2009), ME in Applied Electronics (2011), and PhD in Wireless Communication (2022). As an accredited trainer for Entrepreneurship Educators and Mentors, certified by the Entrepreneurship Development Institute of India, Ahmedabad, he has a proven track record of fostering entrepreneurial skills. He has been recognized through various grants, including Rs. 1,20,000 from the DST NIMAT Project, Government of India, for conducting Entrepreneurship Awareness Camps. He has also received grants from TNSCST, Government of Tamil Nadu, EDI – Tamil Nadu, and MIC, AICTE, for various projects and programs. Additionally, he has recieved Rs. 70,000 from the Centre of Faculty and Professional Development, Anna University, Chennai, for conducting Faculty Development Programs in Data Science and Cybersecurity. He has further enhanced his expertise by participating in AICTE QIP Short-Term Training Programs and other Training programs at prestigious institutions like IIT Bombay, IIT Kharagpur, IISc Bangalore, and ISRO Hyderabad.
Dr.R.Babitha Lincy
M.E, (Ph.D)
D.O.J :  18.07.2022
Areas of Specialization
Image Processing, Deep Learning, Computer Vision
Dr.R.Babitha Lincy is an accomplished academic professional. She has obtained her Ph.D. in Information and communication Engineering from Anna University, in 2022. As an Assistant Professor at Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, she is a dedicated faculty to nurture the next generation of engineers. With over 30 research papers published in SCI journals, Scopus, and international conferences, she has significantly contributed to advancing knowledge in the Deep Learning domain. A strong proponent of continuous learning, she has also obtained NPTEL certifications in Introduction to IoT and Digital Electronics, demonstrating expertise in these cutting-edge areas. As a member of the Institute of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineers (IETE) and the Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE), she actively participates in professional communities, staying updated with the latest industry trends and innovations. She is passionate about combining research with teaching, striving to inspire students and make a lasting impact in academia.
Dr.R.R.Thirrunavukkarasu
M.E, (Ph.D)
D.O.J :  12.06.2024
Areas of Specialization
VLSI Design, Signal and Image Processing, Machine Learning
Dr. R.R. Thirunavukkarasu completed his Doctorate of Philosophy in Bio-signal Processing in 2023, Master’s of Engineering in VLSI Design in 2012, and Bachelor’s of Engineering in Electronics and Communication Engineering in 2008. Throughout his career, he has demonstrated a commitment to research and innovation. He has published five patents and obtained NPTEL certificates in Python for Data Science, CMOS Digital VLSI Design, and Digital Design for Verilog. In 2022, he received NVIDIA Jetson Nano Developer Kits worth Rs.72,000, and in 2024, he secured NVIDIA Jetson Nano Developer and Orion Kits worth Rs. 1,18,000 to set up an industry-supported lab. Additionally, he received a grant of Rs. 75,000 from SERB for conducting an International Workshop on “Antenna Design Techniques for Wireless Power Transfer: Current Trends and Future Prospects”.He is a lifetime member of ISTE and received a certificate as a Toycathon Evaluator.
Ms.G.G.Sreeja
M.E., (Ph.D)
D.O.J : 04.01.2021
Areas of Specialization
Data Structures & algorithms, Software Engineering, Deep Learning
Ms. Sreeja completed her M.E. in 2016, which laid a strong foundation for her career in academia. She began her professional journey in the year 2017, and since then she has consistently contributed to the academia with her interest and expertise in Data Structures, Software engineering and Deep Learning. As an Assistant Professor at Sri Eshwar, she continues her journey to passionately nurture young minds in the field of engineering. Throughout her career, she has actively engaged in continuous professional development, attending more than six Faculty Development Programs (FDPs) related to her areas of expertise. Her passion for research has led her to present more than three papers at IEEE conference proceedings, significantly contributing to her research domain. Additionally, she holds 2 patents, showcasing her commitment to innovation and advancing technology. These experiences reflect her dedication to both education and research in the engineering field.
Ms G.Dency Flora
M.E., (Ph.D)
D.O.J  : 26.08.2021
Areas of Specialization
Signal Processing ,Image Processing & IOT
Ms.G.Dency Flora Completed her Bachelor’s degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering at Sri Ramakrishna Engineering College in 2016. Subsequently she completed her master’s degree in Communication System at PSG College of Technology, Coimbatore in 2018. She started her teaching career in the year 2018. Ms.G.Dency Flora joined Sri Eshwar College of Engineering in the year 2021. She has Completed 5 NPTEL Courses and has published 2 journal papers and 11 conference papers. She fosters academic growth through continuous knowledge enhancement forums like FDPs, online courses and by publishing various research papers.

Ms.N.Banupriya completed her M.E.,in Embedded System Technologies in 2011 and B.E. in Electronics and Communication Engineering in 2009. She has over 12 years of experience in teaching in various institutions. She has successfully completed an Industry collaborative project with GE Healthcare Pvt Ltd, Bangalore in 2019. She has received multiple awards, including the Best Innovative Project Mentor award in Smart India Hackathon and TITAN Wear Your Smarts Hackathon. She has also been an evaluator in AICTE, MHRD’s Toycathon. She has received appreciation awards from Texas Instruments and FOSSEE, IIT Bombay for her exceptional student mentorship. She has attended a 5-day Faculty Development Program organized by the Centre for Development of Technical Education, IIT Kanpur. She holds online certifications from NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute, NPTEL, and Coursera. She is a lifetime member of ISTE.

Ms.P. Megala has a strong academic background and valuable teaching experience. She has completed her M.E. in the year of 2019. After gaining significant knowledge and exposure, working in multiple institutions, she joined Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, in June 2023. In addition to teaching, she is also active in research and innovation. Arun Rangaswamy has an impressive academic and professional background. He has completed his Master’s in Computer Science and Engineering and is pursuing his Doctor of Philosophy at Anna University, Chennai. With more than 15 years of experience in teaching at  various engineering colleges in and around Tamil Nadu, he also served as a Junior Research Fellow in a DST-NRDMS sponsored project entitled “Land Use-Based Flood Damage Assessment: A Case Study of Chennai Floods.” Additionally, he has published more than 35 papers in national and international journals. He has received funding from AICTE – Scheme for Promoting Interests, Creativity, and Ethics among Students (SPICES). His areas of specialization are Geographical Information Systems, Remote Sensing, and Data Analytics.
